phases.title: Rendered production config baseline captured
blockingIssues: Production rendered Nacos baseline has not been captured.
nextActions: Render production Nacos configs from approved production placeholders and attach hash-only evidence.

phases.title: Production or rehearsal Nacos export captured
blockingIssues: Production Nacos export evidence is missing.
nextActions: Export target Nacos dataIds and service registry summary without secret values.

phases.title: Nacos diff reviewed and accepted
blockingIssues: Production Nacos diff review has not been signed.
nextActions: Review rendered-vs-target drift, confirm no Oracle defaults or secret leakage, and attach owner approval.

phases.title: Runtime patch package built and signed
blockingIssues: Signed production runtime patch package evidence is missing.
nextActions: Build or archive the production patch bundle, record its sha256 and attach signature or approval evidence.

phases.title: Publish window and restart sequence approved
blockingIssues: Production publish window and restart sequence approval are missing.
nextActions: Attach approved publish window, restart order and rollback time window.

phases.title: Post-publish Nacos runtime smoke passed
blockingIssues: Post-publish Nacos config and service registry smoke has not passed.
nextActions: Run Nacos config drift smoke against production or rehearsal and attach sanitized report.

phases.title: Post-publish platform smoke passed
blockingIssues: Post-publish platform smoke evidence is missing.
nextActions: Run login, menu, todo and representative runtime page smoke after Nacos publish and runtime restart.

phases.title: Nacos/runtime rollback evidence linked
blockingIssues: Nacos/runtime patch rollback evidence is missing.
nextActions: Attach Nacos restore evidence, previous patch bundle manifest and rollback rehearsal result.

openIssues.description: This example is only a template and cannot be used as production READY evidence.
openIssues.nextAction: Create a real evidence file outside git and run the strict-ready validator.
